contentBlocks.original:
न कर्मणा न प्रजया धनेन त्यागेनैके अमृतत्वमानशुः ।
परेण नाकं निहितं गुहायां विभ्राजते तद्यतयो विशन्ति ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na karmaṇā na prajayā dhanena tyāgenaike amṛtatvamānaśuḥ .
pareṇa nākaṃ nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ vibhrājate tadyatayo viśanti ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: Not by work, nor by progeny or by wealth, but by renunciation alone have some attained immortality. That (immortality) which is even beyond the heaven, is attained by the self-controlled reununciates (as the Self) shining in their heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; कर्मणा (karmaṇā) – by work; न (na) – not; प्रजया (prajayā) – by progeny; धनेन (dhanena) – by wealth; त्यागेन (tyāgena) – by renunciation; एके (eke) – some; अमृतत्वम् (amṛtatvam) – immortality; आनशुः (ānaśuḥ) – attained; परेण (pareṇa) – beyond; नाकम् (nākam) – heaven; निहितम् (nihitam) – placed; गुहायाम् (guhāyām) – in the cave; विभ्राजते (vibhrājate) – shines; तत् (tat) – that; यतयः (yatayaḥ) – ascetics; विशन्ति (viśanti) – enter;

contentBlocks.original:
वेदान्तविज्ञानसुनिश्चितार्थाः संन्यास योगाद्यतय शुद्धसत्त्वाः ।
तेब्रह्मलोके तु परान्तकाले परामृतात्परिमुच्यन्ति सर्वे ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vedāntavijñānasuniścitārthāḥ saṃnyāsa yogādyataya śuddhasattvāḥ .
tebrahmaloke tu parāntakāle parāmṛtātparimucyanti sarve ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: Those who make proper effort and whose minds are pure, who have well-ascertained, clear knowledge of Vedanta, because of a life of sannyasa, they, at the time of the resolution of the individuality in Brahman, are all released from (even) the unmanifest (karma).
contentBlocks.meanings: वेदान्तविज्ञानसुनिश्चितार्थाः (vedāntavijñānasuniścitārthāḥ) – those with well-ascertained Vedanta knowledge (vedānta:Vedanta | vijñāna:knowledge | suniścita:well-ascertained | arthāḥ:those with meaning); संन्यास (saṃnyāsa) – renunciation; योगात् (yogāt) – from yoga; यतय (yatayaḥ) – ascetics; शुद्धसत्त्वाः (śuddhasattvāḥ) – pure-minded (śuddha:pure | sattvāḥ:minded); ते (te) – they; ब्रह्मलोके (brahmaloke) – in the world of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | loke:in the world); तु (tu) – but; परान्तकाले (parāntakāle) – at the end time (parānta:end | kāle:at time); परामृतात् (parāmṛtāt) – from the supreme nectar (para:supreme | amṛtāt:from nectar); परिमुच्यन्ति (parimucyanti) – are released; सर्वे (sarve) – all;

contentBlocks.original:
दह्रं विपापं परमेश्मभूतं यत्पुण्डरीकं पुरमध्यसग्गस्थम् ।
तत्रापि दह्रं गगनं विशोकस्तस्मिन् यदन्तस्तदुपासितव्यम् ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dahraṃ vipāpaṃ parameśmabhūtaṃ yatpuṇḍarīkaṃ puramadhyasaggastham .
tatrāpi dahraṃ gaganaṃ viśokastasmin yadantastadupāsitavyam ..3..
contentBlocks.translation: That, which abides in the city of the body, in the lotus, which is the intellect, and in that small space, that, which is free of all hurt and guilt, free from sorrow, and of the nature of Paramesvara, should be meditated upon.
contentBlocks.meanings: दह्रं (dahraṃ) – small; विपापं (vipāpaṃ) – free of guilt; परमेश्मभूतं (parameśmabhūtaṃ) – of the nature of Paramesvara (parama:supreme | īśma:lord | bhūtaṃ:nature); यत्पुण्डरीकं (yatpuṇḍarīkaṃ) – which is the lotus (yat:which | puṇḍarīkaṃ:lotus); पुरमध्यसग्गस्थम् (puramadhyasaggastham) – abides in the city center (pura:city | madhya:center | sagga:abides | stham:located); तत्रापि (tatrāpi) – even there; दह्रं (dahraṃ) – small; गगनं (gaganaṃ) – sky; विशोकः (viśokaḥ) – free from sorrow; तस्मिन् (tasmin) – in that; यदन्तः (yadantaḥ) – which is inside (yat:which | antaḥ:inside); तदुपासितव्यम् (tadupāsitavyam) – should be meditated upon (tat:that | upāsitavyam:should be meditated upon);

contentBlocks.original:
योवेदादौ स्वरः प्रोक्तो वेदान्ते च प्रतिष्ठितः ।
तस्य प्रकृतिलीनस्य यः परः स महेश्वरः ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yovedādau svaraḥ prokto vedānte ca pratiṣṭhitaḥ .
tasya prakṛtilīnasya yaḥ paraḥ sa maheśvaraḥ ..4..
contentBlocks.translation: The one who is the syllable (om) that is uttered at the beginning of the Veda and is well-established in the upanishads, the one who is the cause, the truth of that causal principle which is resolved (in himself), he is the Lord, Mahesvara.
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – who; वेद (veda) – Veda; आदौ (ādau) – at the beginning; स्वरः (svaraḥ) – syllable; प्रोक्तः (proktaḥ) – uttered; वेदान्ते (vedānte) – in the Upanishads (veda:Veda | ante:end); च (ca) – and; प्रतिष्ठितः (pratiṣṭhitaḥ) – established; तस्य (tasya) – of that; प्रकृतिलीनस्य (prakṛtilīnasya) – resolved in nature (prakṛti:nature | līnasya:resolved); यः (yaḥ) – who; परः (paraḥ) – supreme; स (sa) – he; महेश्वरः (maheśvaraḥ) – Lord Mahesvara (mahā:great | īśvaraḥ:Lord);
